payload.answer: Knowledge graphs model information as a network of entities connected by relationships, enabling queries that reflect real-world semantics (e.g., 'Person works_at Company'). They support properties (attributes), typing of entities, and rich edge semantics. Common components include nodes (entities), edges (relationships), labels, and sometimes provenance and confidence. To learn more, start with fundamental concepts (nodes, edges, labels), typical data models (RDF subject-predicate-object triples, property graphs), storage options (triple stores, graph databases), querying languages (SPARQL for RDF, Cypher/GQL for property graphs), and common use cases (recommendations, fraud detection, knowledge integration).

entity_resolution.reason: No specific entity queries provided; treating as a general knowledge graph inquiry.

memory_cards.statement: A knowledge graph represents information as entities (nodes) and their relationships (edges), enabling semantic queries and reasoning.
